title: Implement RX2 dual receive UI and routing
description: Add RX2 controls for VFO/frequency operation, split the panadapter when RX2 is enabled, and support audio monitor modes for RX1, RX2, or both. Reference Thetis RX2 behavior from C:\Users\Admin\Desktop\Thetis-master.
close_reason: Implemented RX2/VFO B state, DSP dual receive routing, split panadapter UI, and RX audio source controls.

title: Stabilize auto-notch validation and lock
description: Refine the ANF-driven auto-notch tracker so it dynamically samples candidate center/width stability, validates before emitting a notch, locks verified notches to stable center/width values, and rejects voice-like wandering energy to reduce false positives.
notes: Also gate NotchOverlay resize handles on the NOTCH armed state so existing notch bands do not intercept normal panadapter/waterfall clicks unless the operator explicitly enables notch editing.
close_reason: Auto-notch tracker now dynamically samples candidate stability, locks verified center/width values, rejects wandering voice-like candidates before validation, and NotchOverlay editing is gated on NOTCH armed state. Targeted frontend tests passed; full typecheck is still blocked by existing unused-symbol errors in signal-estimator.ts.

title: Implement real HDR waterfall extraction logic
description: User feedback: POP and normal waterfall still look essentially the same. Implement performant signal-processing logic, not just color/shader changes: adaptive dynamic-range mapping, multi-scale local contrast, coherence-aware weak-signal extraction, and a distinct normal HDR waterfall path.
notes: Implemented continuous waterfall scroll speed instead of row cadence; full-rate row ingest with shader-side fractional scroll speed, larger history texture, stronger normal-mode dB-space floor tuck/terrain lift, POP anti-clipping, and explicit terrain shadow/rim shader cues. Focused tests/build pass. Latest diagnostics endpoint was reachable but blocked because the desktop process was disconnected from WDSP/audio after restart.

title: Make POP waterfall depth visually falsifiable
description: User still sees no visible difference after the relief shader/default migration. Replace subtle per-pixel lighting with an unmistakable displaced outline/offset glow treatment that is obvious on live waterfall screenshots, and verify the live bundle/settings again.
notes: Scope widened after user feedback: normal waterfall should also get cutting-edge dynamic range/relief, while POP remains the stronger weak-signal extraction mode. Implement baseline HDR lift plus subtle edge relief; POP gets stronger alpha-visible displaced outline.
close_reason: Made the waterfall visual change falsifiable: normal mode now has HDR lift plus subtle relief, POP has alpha-visible offset shadow/outline glow, live app serves index-C5LXRaha.js with the new shader, POP prefs restored, Waterfall tests and frontend build passed.

Starting accepted trace live159 (tmp/20260616T115625648Z-nr5-live159-20m-14335-fresh-accepted-state): ready 100/100, trend rx-leveler-settling-watch, NR5 input avg -39.030 dBFS, output avg -52.334 dBFS, output movement 55.3 dB, probability/confidence/gate avg 0.170/0.318/0.483, recovery avg 0.260, weakRecovered 24, weakNearTarget 25, weakDropout 12, weakBelowInput 68, lowEvidenceLift 4, lowEvidenceSuppressed 35, leveler boostSlew 26, peakLimited 3.

Added a narrow native weak-recovery floor after low-evidence suppression and before AGC continuity memory. First candidate hash 0EF19C8BE604BA5B5B2A94EF351A5B6B9100C70D4AC246166A6BD59151B48968 passed focused tests, but live160 (tmp/20260616T120320294Z-nr5-live160-20m-14335-recovery-floor) was too broad: ready 100/100, trend nr5-output-level-watch, output avg improved to -46.284 dBFS and weakDropout improved 12->6, but lowEvidenceLift rose 4->8 and lowEvidenceSuppressed fell 35->10. Rejected/tightened this candidate to avoid raising noise-only or ambiguous floor.

Tightened the floor drive in native/wdsp/spnr.c by raising confidence/probability/recovery/AGC gates, strengthening the low-evidence veto, and reducing floor target/lift cap/blend. Final deployed win-x64 wdsp.dll hash 5E937F3AC90729483ED97BBA8AE8FB29295632AE04760B4079ADA7560B2EBB13 across runtime and test locations.

Final evidence live161 (tmp/20260616T120633350Z-nr5-live161-20m-14335-recovery-floor-tightened): ready 100/100, trend nr5-output-level-watch, NR5 input avg -39.703 dBFS, output avg -48.995 dBFS, output movement 55.5 dB, probability/confidence/gate avg 0.176/0.313/0.455, recovery avg 0.258, weakRecovered 22, weakNearTarget 22, weakDropout 6, weakBelowInput 61, lowEvidenceLift 3, lowEvidenceSuppressed 23, leveler boostSlew 34, peakLimited 4, outputLimited 0. Interpretation: accepted as the safer final-pass candidate because it halves weak dropout versus live159 while keeping low-evidence lift at/below prior accepted state; remaining output-level watch is downstream leveler settling across real speech/silence gaps on the live signal, not unchecked NR5 peak limiting.

Verification passed: native CMake build passed; dotnet test tests/Zeus.Dsp.Tests/Zeus.Dsp.Tests.csproj filtered to NoiseReductionTests/Nr5SyntheticFixtureTests/WdspNativeLoaderTests passed 145, skipped 120; dotnet test tests/Zeus.Server.Tests/Zeus.Server.Tests.csproj filtered to DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests/DspLiveDiagnosticsServiceTests passed 37; npm --prefix zeus-web run build passed with existing Vite chunk/dynamic import warnings; git diff --check passed on touched NR5/native/server paths with LF->CRLF warnings only. Backend left running on https://localhost:6443, connected P2 192.168.1.25:1024, USB 14.335 MHz, NR5 active.
Continuation NR5 live refinement: targeted downstream RX leveler fade-up after NR5-muted gaps on 20m 14.335 MHz USB.

Fresh NR5 baseline live162 (tmp/20260616T121755760Z-nr5-live162-20m-14335-continuation-baseline): ready 100/100, trend rx-leveler-settling-watch, audio avg -47.234 dBFS / movement 68.9 dB, leveler input avg -59.492 dBFS, applied gain avg 12.362 dB / movement 36.0 dB, boostSlew 24, peakLimited 1, hold min 0 / avg 14.74, NR5 input avg -37.291 dBFS, NR5 output avg -57.581 dBFS / movement 62.8 dB, weakRecovered 7, weakDropout 10, lowEvidenceLift 1, lowEvidenceSuppressed 37, alignmentMismatch 40. Interpretation: NR5 still avoids broad noise lifting, but below-gate cleaned gaps let the final leveler gain memory decay; the next weak above-gate block then audibly fades upward.

Matched NR2/EMNR reference live162 (tmp/20260616T121856022Z-nr2-live162-20m-14335-reference): ready 80/80, trend audio-level-watch, audio avg -22.914 dBFS / movement 20.6 dB, leveler input avg -44.569 dBFS, applied gain avg 21.740 dB, boostSlew 22, hold min/avg 18/18. NR2 remains steadier mainly because EMNR keeps the leveler input floor high enough that hold never drops, but that behavior is not suitable as a direct NR5 floor-lift because it raises more noise.

Applied managed-only RX leveler refinement in Zeus.Server.Hosting/DspPipelineService.cs: kept true below-gate blocks at 0 dB applied gain, softened post-hold gain-memory decay from 6.0 to 4.5 dB/block, and added a memory-catchup release path using the existing very-fast 5.5 dB/block only when a cresty weak above-gate block arrives with retained positive gain memory, enough RMS/peak evidence, and at least 10 dB peak headroom. Added DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests coverage proving a long muted gap is not lifted while the following weak cresty speech block catches up by 5.5 dB.

Verification: focused server diagnostics/audio tests passed 38/38; npm --prefix zeus-web run build passed with existing Vite chunk/dynamic-import warnings; git diff --check for the touched managed/test/beads files passed with LF->CRLF warnings only. Native DSP was not rebuilt because this pass did not change native code; deployed NR5 native hash remains 5E937F3AC90729483ED97BBA8AE8FB29295632AE04760B4079ADA7560B2EBB13 from the prior accepted pass.

Compared with live162 baseline: boostSlew improved 24->15, alignmentMismatch 40->28, audio avg improved -47.234->-45.638, audio movement improved 68.9->64.3, NR5 output movement improved 62.8->58.1, lowEvidenceLift stayed 1 and lowEvidenceSuppressed improved 37->46. Weak-dropout counts are not a clean A/B due changed live RF content, so issue remains open. Backend left running on https://localhost:6443 with P2 192.168.1.25:1024, USB 14.335 MHz, requested/effective NR5.
2026-06-16 NR5 finish pass on 20m / 14.335 USB:
- Kept the latest native gate-stable whisper-floor refinement in native/wdsp/spnr.c. It targets borderline weak speech with stable AGC gate + mask history while vetoing low-evidence noise through probability/recovery/mask and lowEvidenceNoiseDrive gates.
- Built native WDSP and deployed wdsp.dll to runtime/test output locations. SHA256: 84F379E09E2B2D22288F524957C8E8DF2B61BCFE8D8DA429471A7ED72C2140E9.
- Focused tests passed after code changes: dotnet test tests\Zeus.Dsp.Tests\Zeus.Dsp.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~NoiseReductionTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nr5SyntheticFixtureTests|FullyQualifiedName~WdspNativeLoaderTests" => 145 passed, 120 skipped; dotnet test tests\Zeus.Server.Tests\Zeus.Server.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests|FullyQualifiedName~DspLiveDiagnosticsServiceTests" => 38 passed. npm --prefix zeus-web run build passed with existing Vite warnings.
- Live165 post-native validation: tmp\20260616T123757175Z-nr5-live165-20m-14335-gate-stable-whisper, 120/120 ready, trend nr5-output-level-watch, audio avg -27.313 dBFS/move 63.3, leveler input avg -44.617, leveler gain avg 17.463, boost-slew 23, peak-limited 5, output-limited 0, NR5 out avg -44.698/move 57.0, weak recovered/dropout/below-input 33/14/56, low-evidence lifted/suppressed 2/15, alignment mismatch 55. This did not trip the reject condition because low-evidence lifts remained low and limiter stayed off.
- Live166 sanity after learner settled: tmp\20260616T123952109Z-nr5-live166-20m-14335-gate-stable-whisper-sanity, 100/100 ready, trend rx-leveler-settling-watch, audio avg -33.275/move 65.8, leveler input avg -42.677, leveler gain avg 9.559, boost-slew 12, peak-limited 2, output-limited 0, NR5 input/output avg -31.302/-38.908, weak recovered/dropout/below-input 7/15/26, low-evidence lifted/suppressed 0/7, alignment mismatch 22. This supports keeping the native whisper-floor: it did not lift low-evidence noise and alignment improved versus live165.
- Current known residual: NR5 is much better than the early continuation baseline, and output is protected, but live diagnostics still show rx-leveler-settling/nr5-output-level-watch on deep weak/quiet transitions. Further work should focus on distinguishing real speech pauses from residual weak syllables at the leveler/diagnostic alignment boundary, not broadening low-evidence native lifts.
2026-06-16 NR5 continuation / crest-qualified leveler catch-up:
- Fresh baseline live167 on 14.335 USB NR5 after the native whisper-floor change: tmp\20260616T124432063Z-nr5-live167-20m-14335-post-whisper-settling, 140/140 ready, trend rx-leveler-settling-watch, audio avg/move -29.697/64.0 dBFS, leveler input avg/move -41.906/58.0, applied gain avg/move 12.323/36.0, boost-slew 26, peak-limited 2, output-limited 0, NR5 input/output avg -33.266/-37.187, weak recovered/dropout/below-input 69/15/59, low-evidence lifted/suppressed 2/20, alignment mismatch 40. Per-sample inspection showed many boost-limited blocks were weak cresty post-NR audio with ample peak headroom (e.g. RMS around -48 to -60 dBFS, peak around -39 to -49 dBFS, headroom >25 dB), not flat floor.
- Implemented a scoped managed RX leveler refinement in DspPipelineService: a crest-qualified catch-up tier at 7.5 dB/block. It only engages when current gain memory exists, post-NR RMS is within -66..-28 dBFS, input peak >= -52 dBFS, crest >= 8 dB, peak headroom >= 16 dB, and desired-current gain gap >= 6 dB. Flat deep-floor content remains on the conservative 3.5 dB path; normal very-fast path remains 5.5 dB/block.
- Updated DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests so the cresty weak-speech-after-gap test expects 7.5 dB catch-up while the flat deep-floor test remains 3.5 dB.
- Verification: focused server tests passed after stopping the locked backend: dotnet test tests\Zeus.Server.Tests\Zeus.Server.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests|FullyQualifiedName~DspLiveDiagnosticsServiceTests" => 38 passed. git diff --check for touched NR5/leveler files reported only LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings.
- Live168 immediately after restart was discarded as evidence because it was blocked/incomplete (118/140 ready, 6 failed) while the backend/radio path was still settling. It hinted boost-slew reduction but is not used as acceptance proof.
- Clean validation live169 on 14.335 USB NR5: tmp\20260616T125317369Z-nr5-live169-20m-14335-crest-catchup-clean, 120/120 ready, trend rx-leveler-settling-watch, audio avg/move -41.443/64.1, leveler input avg/move -56.390/58.2, applied gain avg/move 15.100/36.0, boost-slew 17, peak-limited 6, output-limited 0, weak recovered/dropout/below-input 19/12/88, low-evidence lifted/suppressed 3/42. Band content was much quieter than live167, but boost-slew was lower and output limiting stayed off.
- Quick 20m scan found 14.325 MHz USB currently stronger than 14.335 (5/5 fresh samples, audio avg -18 dBFS, NR5 confidence avg 0.401/probability 0.250). Moved to 14.325 for active validation.
- Active validation live170 on 14.325 USB NR5: tmp\20260616T125634378Z-nr5-live170-20m-14325-active-crest-catchup, 120/120 ready, trend nr5-output-level-watch, audio avg/move -30.979/64.6, leveler input avg/move -42.843/58.3, applied gain avg/move 11.972/36.0, boost-slew 17, peak-limited 3, output-limited 0, NR5 input/output avg -30.944/-40.326, weak recovered/dropout/below-input 14/5/43, low-evidence lifted/suppressed 0/17, alignment mismatch 42. Interpretation: leveler safety held, no low-evidence noise was lifted on the active spot, but NR5 still has weak/strong output gap on mixed active/quiet content.
- Same-frequency NR2 reference live171: tmp\20260616T125812303Z-nr2-live171-20m-14325-active-reference, 80/80 ready, trend audio-level-watch, audio avg/move -20.145/16.4, leveler input avg/move -38.703/42.1, applied gain avg/move 18.746/35.6, boost-slew 15, peak-limited 1, output-limited 0. NR2 sounds fuller because it holds the downstream leveler hotter, but it does not provide NR5 low-evidence/weak-signal discrimination. NR5 was restored afterward and live diagnostics report requested/effective NR5.
- Current stopping point: keep the crest-qualified catch-up refinement. It moves leveler settling in the right direction without broadening native NR5 low-evidence opening. Remaining work is weak/strong normalization on mixed active/quiet content; any next change should use matched active traces or offline aligned audio, because live alignment mismatch remains significant.

After patch live177: NR5 audio movement 5.1 dB, lowEvidenceLift 0, outputLimiter 0, weakBelowInput 2, no hard-collapse rows; NR2 same window 6.9 dB. Quiet 14.175 check live178 showed NR5 suppressing floor far below no NR/NR2 (no NR -18.0 dBFS, NR2 -24.9, NR5 -56.6 avg), though that short trace had transient low-evidence lifts; settled follow-up live179 had lowEvidenceLift 0 after signal content returned. comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
NR5 behavior refinement pass complete for 14.230 MHz USB live diagnostics.

Implemented:
- native/wdsp/spnr.c: added NR5 speech-valley continuity support with a stricter core path plus a weak-tail path gated by confidence, AGC gate, weak-signal memory, recovery drive, mask smoothing, and low-evidence suppression. Goal is to preserve syllable tails/weak fragments without globally lifting the floor.
- Zeus.Server.Hosting/DspPipelineService.cs: made the RX audio leveler NR5-aware in two ways: it no longer restores an NR5-muted noise floor, and uncertain NR5 weak-tail frames now get an evidence-weighted boost ceiling so they do not request full global leveler gain.
- tests/Zeus.Server.Tests/DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests.cs: added coverage for NR5 muted floor suppression and uncertain speech-tail boost capping.
- Deployed final win-x64 wdsp.dll SHA256 c0ae8ca31918a02b99844271636242ad21db2ade7d5b120c6dc951fe7b4dce5f.

Live evidence:
- live182 baseline after squelch-off: weakRecovered 42/70, weakDropout 19, weakBelowInput 9, lowEvidenceLifted 3/3, levelerMax 29.9 dB.
- live183 with NR5-aware leveler: weakRecovered 43/70, weakDropout 18, weakBelowInput 2, lowEvidenceLifted 0/3, levelerMax 22.2 dB.
- live184 with first speech-valley native change: weakRecovered 36/70, weakDropout 18, weakBelowInput 2, lowEvidenceLifted 0/2, alignment mismatch improved to 4.3%, levelerMax 21.2 dB.
- live186 with continuity-tail native tune during a lower-probability window: weakDropout improved to 4, weakNearTarget 35, but lowEvidenceLifted 4/17 and levelerMax 25.6 dB showed the leveler needed a tighter uncertainty cap.
- final live187 after managed evidence cap: 70/70 ready, squelch off, no output limiting, lowEvidenceLifted 0/21, weakDropout 4, levelerMax 20.5 dB, constrained leveler samples 5. This trace was a weaker/less speech-like window (probAvg 0.167, confAvg 0.312, gateAvg 0.501), so it is mainly a noise-floor guardrail rather than proof of full weak-voice recovery.

Verification:
- dotnet test tests/Zeus.Server.Tests/Zeus.Server.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests|FullyQualifiedName~DspLiveDiagnosticsServiceTests" -p:UseSharedCompilation=false passed 40/40.
- dotnet test tests/Zeus.Dsp.Tests/Zeus.Dsp.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~NoiseReductionTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nr5SyntheticFixtureTests|FullyQualifiedName~WdspNativeLoaderTests" -p:UseSharedCompilation=false passed 145, skipped 120.
- tools/audit-wdsp-runtime-artifacts.ps1 -FailOnMissingWinX64Nr5 -JsonOnly passed; win-x64 NR5 ready with final hash c0ae8ca3..., non-win-x64 RIDs still pending NR5 rebuild as expected.
- tools/audit-wdsp-native-symbols.ps1 -BinaryPath Zeus.Dsp/runtimes/win-x64/native/wdsp.dll -RequireBinaryExports -JsonOnly passed: readyForReview=true, binaryExportCount=539, no missing required exports, no signature mismatches.
- git diff --check on touched behavior/test files passed with LF/CRLF warnings only.

Issue remains in progress. Current best interpretation: NR5 now avoids re-amplifying low-evidence noise and preserves more weak-tail fragments, but matched-window speech evidence is still needed before claiming the weak-signal voice recovery target is solved.

title: Refine NR5 spectral hold to reduce remaining fade
description: After anti-fade pass, long-term AGC movement is reduced but live NR5 output still dips when spectral gain collapses between weak coherent frames. Add a bounded recent-signal spectral hold that preserves faint signal continuity without lifting noise-only frames.
close_reason: Refined NR5 weak-signal recovery and anti-fade behavior: added signal-evidence-driven AGC recovery and bounded fast makeup, tightened narrow-tone false-open guard, deployed tested WDSP DLL, and captured live diagnostics at tmp/nr5-final-weak-recovery-live.json showing output span 21.3 dB over a 25.9 dB input span with no -60 dBFS collapse. Tests: NoiseReductionTests.Wdsp_Nr5 4 passed; NoiseReductionTests|WdspNativeLoaderTests 138 passed / 120 skipped.

title: Make Pop Relief unmistakable in live waterfall
description: User still sees no visual difference with POP active. Confirm live settings and replace subtle relief color-grade with an obvious GPU emboss/halo/depth treatment that is visibly different at default settings.
close_reason: Made Pop Relief unmistakable in the live waterfall: stronger GPU bevel/halo shader, legacy 48/42 relief migration to 92/64, persisted live prefs updated, focused frontend/backend tests and builds passed.

title: Fix SetTXACFCOMPprofile P/Invoke signature mismatch
description: The new WDSP native symbol audit reports SetTXACFCOMPprofile as managed 7 params versus native 5 params in native/wdsp/cfcomp.c. Fix the C# binding/call surface to match the vendored WDSP/Thetis ABI without changing DSP defaults, then rerun the audit and focused/full tests.
close_reason: Fixed SetTXACFCOMPprofile P/Invoke to match the vendored WDSP 5-parameter ABI, removed the stale Q-pointer call/comment, reran the native symbol audit to readyForReview=true, and verified Zeus.Dsp.Tests plus full solution build/test.

title: Make Pop Relief waterfall visibly stronger and diagnose crash

close_reason: Fixed: TX start now clears Audio Suite audition/TX Monitor, with server and frontend store coverage.

title: Catch wide coherent auto-notch blockers
description: Enhance the auto-notch detector so ANF-driven auto notching catches strong, coherent wide blockers visible in live panadapter/waterfall diagnostics, including edge blockers and paired mid-span lines, without turning ordinary broad occupied signals into auto notches.
close_reason: Auto notch detector now accepts strong coherent wide blockers and edge-visible blockers while preserving broad real-signal rejection; targeted tests and typecheck pass.

title: Stop NR5 audio fading in and out
description: NR5 audio still audibly fades in/out after weak/strong leveler changes. Stabilize post-NR leveler/gate/limiter so confirmed signals hold steady without pumping, while noise-only frames are not lifted.
close_reason: NR5 anti-fade pass: added held level-drive state, slowed gate/envelope/gain motion, decoupled limiter from long-term AGC gain, softened weak-island spectral gain drops, rebuilt WDSP, verified NR5 and broader DSP slices, and sampled live P2 diagnostics with squelch/ANF isolated.

title: Make NR5 weak and strong signals equal loudness
description: NR5 post-normalizer still leaves weak and powerful signals at noticeably different loudness. Refine AGC/leveler so confirmed signals normalize toward the same perceived volume without reintroducing pumping or lifting noise-only frames.
close_reason: NR5 leveler now uses a confirmed-signal constant RMS target, 48x makeup ceiling, weak-island spectral protection, slew-limited gain, and final RMS limiter; tested with NR5 DSP slice, broader DSP slice, and live P2 diagnostics.

The verification needs to validate the payload using the published runtime context or otherwise resolve dependencies from the installer layout.

title: Stabilize NR5 output AGC pumping

close_reason: Stabilized NR5 post-normalizer pumping: added smoothed AGC RMS envelope, slower gate decay/hang, slower gain recovery, compressed gate drive, and capped NR5 makeup gain. Verified native WDSP rebuild, focused NR5 tests, broader NoiseReduction/WdspNativeLoader slice, and live P2 diagnostics with NR5 active on 192.168.1.25:1024. Live agcGain span dropped from 2.78 before fix to 0.82 in the post-fix sample.

Fixed SQL is enforced inside WDSP, while the backend adaptive gate state only applies to DYN SQL, so diagnostics must expose the gate source and avoid using adaptive gate internals as fixed SQL open/closed evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RX audio diagnostics expose squelch mode, gate source, and whether the open state is known; fixed SQL silence recommendations warn that WDSP fixed squelch may be closed; squelch diagnostics report backend gate fields consistently in fixed mode; focused backend and frontend tests cover the live mismatch.

notes: Patched RX audio leveler to ramp upward gain changes across each audio block using a multiplicative gain step, avoiding block-boundary gain jumps that can sound like light crackle while keeping immediate cuts for loud arrivals. Added DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests.ApplyRxAudioLeveler_RampsBoostAcrossBlock. Focused no-build test passed: dotnet test tests/Zeus.Server.Tests/Zeus.Server.Tests.csproj --no-build --filter FullyQualifiedName~DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests (16 passed). title: fix(tci): iq_samplerate echoes requested rate instead of actual delivery rate
description: Issue #570: HandleIqSampleRate echoes the client-requested rate verbatim even though the IQ stream is always delivered at the hardware sample rate. Clients that size their DSP pipeline on the echoed value silently get the wrong rate. Path A fix: echo the hardware rate (radio.Snapshot().SampleRate) so clients detect that their request wasn't honored. Same honesty fix applied to audio_samplerate (always 48000). The stored _iqSampleRate is kept for future Path B decimation.
close_reason: HandleIqSampleRate and HandleAudioSampleRate now echo the actual delivery rate; all tests green

title: feat(cw): decode CW server-side from RxAudioAvailable, stream text over hub (headless + desktop)
description: Pivot from PR1's client-side WebAudio decoder (broken: doesn't work in desktop/Photino mode where audio is rendered natively, and can't run headless). Move decoding to the server: a CwDecoderService (BackgroundService, singleton+hosted) subscribes to DspPipelineService.RxAudioAvailable (demodulated post-AGC RX audio, ~30Hz ticks, headless-safe — fires pre-sink regardless of any audio output). Runs an envelope-based key detector (energy variation above an adaptively-tracked noise floor, with Schmitt hysteresis — works for CW-on-SSB where there is no true silence between elements) + Morse FSM, gated on RxMode.CWL/CWU. Emits decoded text + wpm/snr/confidence via a new CwDecodedTextFrame (MsgType 0x31) broadcast by StreamingHub, mirroring the existing CwEngineStatusFrame (0x30) pattern. Frontend: ws-client handler dispatches to the existing cw-decoder-store.appendChar; client decoder.ts/useCwDecoderAudio.ts deleted. RED-LIGHT: touches Zeus.Contracts wire format + adds a service → needs maintainer (Brian/EI6LF) sign-off before merge. Supersedes zeus-67t (client timing fix).
close_reason: Delivered by PR #549 (cw-decoder-548 → develop): CwDecoderService + CwAudioDecoder server-side envelope/Morse decode on RxAudioAvailable, CwDecodedTextFrame (MsgType 0x31) over StreamingHub, ws-client dispatch to cw-decoder-store, client decoder deleted. Merged to develop.

title: fix(cw): decoder timing must derive from sample count, not performance.now()
description: Live validation on feature/cw-decoder-investigation: the CW decoder only emits 1-2 element chars (E/T/I), reports a pinned ~59 WPM (ditMs clamped to the 10ms floor), and shows ~1dB SNR. Root cause: CwDecoder.process() timestamps every sample with performance.now(); processSamples() iterates an entire ~960-sample frame in a microsecond burst, so all samples in a frame share one timestamp and dit/dah durations measure ~0. Fix: thread a sample-based clock (sampleIndex/sampleRate*1000) and rework the per-sample debounce accordingly. Secondary: AdaptiveThreshold cold-start (noiseFloor=0) reports everything as keyed for the first window, emitting a spurious 'E' on every on/off toggle — add a primed guard. Deferred (#3): block-based Goertzel + slower noise-floor tracker if per-sample magnitude proves too noisy.
close_reason: Superseded by zeus-dmx: decoding moves server-side; the sample-clock timing insight ports to C#.

title: nightly macOS signing fails: missing secrets: inherit in nightly.yml
description: The Nightly workflow (.github/workflows/nightly.yml) calls release.yml as a reusable workflow (uses:) without 'secrets: inherit'. release.yml's macOS signing step (gated on is-nightly=='true') reads secrets.MACOS_CERTIFICATE / _PWD / KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD, but a reusable-workflow call only sees repo secrets when the caller inherits them — so they arrive empty and 'security import' fails with 'SecKeychainItemImport: Unable to decode the provided data' (empty base64 .p12). Confirmed the secrets exist at repo level. Fix: add 'secrets: inherit' to the nightly job. Lives on main (schedule honours default branch only). Run: actions/runs/26388557106 job 77673178545.
close_reason: Merged to develop via PR #528 (secrets: inherit in nightly.yml)

title: fix(protocol-1): include TxFreq in non-MOX phase rotation so HL2 extamp boards emit Kenwood CAT on dial change
description:
GitHub issue: https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/issues/361

## Symptom

Square SDR 2 (HL2-class, fw 75.52, classifies as HermesLite2) outputs no band voltage on ACC-pin-1 BVO under Zeus, regardless of the Band Volts (C3 bit 3) checkbox state. Same hardware + same connector follows the dial fine under deskHPSDR with the checkbox in either state. Operator: Colin (@41south), driving an Xiegu XPA125B.

## Root cause

Square SDR 2's gateware enables the upstream HL2 `extamp` module, which emits Kenwood CAT (`FA000XXXXXXXX;`) on `io_uart_txd` only when the FPGA's internal TX-frequency register changes value. A daughter STM32G031 parses that CAT on USART2 and drives BVO PWM (TIM2 CH4) + BCD GPIO out to the rear-panel ACC connector.

Zeus's `PhaseRegisters(phase, mox=false, psArmed=false)` in `Zeus.Protocol1/Protocol1Client.cs:965-971` rotates only `Config / RxFreq / DriveFilter / Attenuator`. `CcRegister.TxFreq` (C0=0x02) is **never** written while receiving — only when MOX is asserted (lines 957-963). Result: the FPGA's TX-NCO register stays static during RX dial changes, no CAT edge fires, BVO never updates.

deskHPSDR's `src/old_protocol.c:2837-2846` writes `output_buffer[C0] = 0x02` (DUC frequency) on every pass of its round-robin scheduler regardless of MOX state, which is what keeps the FPGA `extamp` edge alive.

## Fix sketch

Add `CcRegister.TxFreq` to the non-MOX, non-PS-armed rotation phase table so the TX-NCO is refreshed on every dial change while receiving. Mirror to the `psArmed && !mox` table (`Protocol1Client.cs:933-951`) if not already covered.

## Risk / red-light points

The Protocol-1 round-robin is load-bearing for every supported board (Metis, Hermes, ANAN, Orion-MkII family, HL2). Before merging:

1. Verify the new rotation doesn't displace a phase a non-HL2 board depends on.
2. Cross-check against mi0bot Thetis and ramdor Thetis that they also write TX-NCO during RX.
3. Bench-test on HL2 at 28400 RX-only to confirm no regression (no spurious BVO change, no panadapter glitch, no PS-arm misbehavior).
4. PR description must flag "scheduler change — non-HL2 boards untested locally, please bench-verify before merge" for maintainer.

## Out of scope

- BCD on ACC pins 11-14 — Colin offered to test once BVO works. Fix should light up both at once (same `extamp` → STM32 chain), but bench-confirm before claiming.
- C3 bit 3 (`EnableHl2BandVolts`) wire-write — already correct per audit; no change needed.

## Test plan

- HL2 RX: panadapter on 28400, dial sweep across band; no spurious behavior.
- HL2 MOX (dummy load only, 28400): no rotation regression; PA/drive cadence unchanged.
- Code read: confirm non-HL2 boards (Metis / Hermes / Orion-MkII variant) still receive a `Config` phase often enough not to lose nrx / dither / random settings.
- Ship a pre-release build for Colin to test on Square SDR 2 + XPA125B.

title: P1: Implement onboard codec audio path (Hermes RX headphones + TX mic-in)
description:
Reported in GH #426 against Hermes on v0.8.2: 'Onboard codec not worked'. Thetis / linHPSDR / piHPSDR all work on the same board.

Root cause (confirmed in code):
- Zeus.Protocol1/ControlFrame.cs:516-532, 562 — EP2 outbound 8-byte slot is [L s16 BE][R s16 BE][I s16 BE][Q s16 BE], and the L/R audio bytes are intentionally left zero. Comment: 'HL2 has no audio codec in the MVP target, so audio bytes stay zero'.
- Zeus.Protocol1/PacketParser.cs:240 — incoming mic samples at off+6,off+7 are read but discarded ('Unused for RX MVP').

On HL2 this was acceptable because operators use host audio (browser getUserMedia + WebAudio). On Hermes / Mercury / ANAN-class the radio's onboard codec IS the primary audio path — operators expect to plug a mic into the front-panel jack and listen on the radio's headphone jack. That entire path is unimplemented.

This is the headline failure in #426 and the most likely reason linoobs's Hermes is unusable in Zeus while it works fine in Thetis.
design: Gate via BoardCapabilitiesTable. Hermes / Mercury / ANAN-class default to onboard codec; HL2 defaults to host/browser audio. TX path: route mic source samples into EP2 L/R bytes (currently always zero in ControlFrame.WriteUsbFrame). RX path: extract the 16-bit mic-monitor bytes in PacketParser.TryParsePacket and surface via an IRxCodecAudioSource seam (parallel to IRxAudioSink for DSP-out). Operator UI toggle to override the per-board default. Coordinate with audio-native plan ([[project_audio_native_plan]]).
acceptance_criteria: On a Hermes board with mic plugged into the front-panel jack, transmitted SSB audio matches Thetis behaviour. RX audio plays out the front-panel headphone jack at a usable level. Behaviour is board-gated via BoardCapabilitiesTable so HL2 default (browser mic) is preserved.
notes: Phase A landed in commit (fix(p1) on fix/426-onboard-codec branch) — RX audio reaches the radio's headphone jack on Hermes / ANAN-class / OrionMkII / G2E boards via EP2 L/R bytes. HL2 unchanged (HasOnboardCodec=false). Phase B (radio mic → WDSP TXA) tracked in new bd item — see /api/recent for that item's ID; needs operator UI for mic-source selection.

title: Audio start does not load selected profile
description: Starting audio with a saved profile should load that profile's configured input/output state. Currently audio startup appears to ignore or misapply the selected profile.
notes:
Reopened after bench report: first transmission still starts before the selected TX/audio profile is applied. Investigating startup/connect path instead of manual selector path.
Final runtime check after process handoff: live OpenhpsdrZeus PID 26256 is listening on 127.0.0.1:6061 and connected to 192.168.1.25:1024 in LSB at 384 kHz. VST route remains active and master bypass is false. A final 60-sample Audio Suite chain meter check showed OUT min -43.4 dB, max -36.4 dB, and 0 near-full-scale samples (> -6 dB).
close_reason:
Fixed and verified.

TX/audio profile activation now runs outside the TX profile panel and is awaited by the first MOX/TUNE/PTT/key preflight before TX is keyed, so the selected station profile and saved Audio Suite profile are applied before first transmission.

VST meter logic was tightened so off-air live preview degraded/pass-through blocks no longer overwrite the last real VST engine OUT meter, while on-air TX still records honest pass-through meters if the engine degrades.

Live verification after rebuild/relaunch on 127.0.0.1:6061:
- Connected to 192.168.1.25:1024 at 48 kHz.
- Audio Suite route: vst, engineActive: true, masterBypassed: false.
- TX fidelity policy: essb, Audio Suite profile: VST x1.
- State mic gain -4 dB and leveler max 11 dB match the essb station profile.
- LSB TX filter is signed correctly: -4000..-40 Hz.
- 90 Audio Suite chain meter samples while connected: OUT min -43.5 dB, max -34.0 dB, 0 near-full-scale samples (> -6 dB). The prior fast OUT jump to about -1 dB did not recur.

Checks:
- Focused frontend TX profile tests: 16 passed.
- Frontend production build: passed.
- Frontend lint: passed with existing warnings.
- OpenhpsdrZeus backend build: passed 0 warnings / 0 errors.

Full-suite caveat: current dirty worktree has unrelated verification blockers filed as zeus-4lbg (Waterfall Vitest expectation and DisplayIntelligenceSettingsDto test constructor compile error), so full Vitest/dotnet test suites are not fully green in this worktree.

title: Design experimental NR5 weak-signal noise reduction
description:
Design and implement an experimental NR5 noise-reduction mode for weak-signal receive work. The goal is not to expose the existing RNNoise/NR3 path under a new name; NR3 is intentionally hidden because its generic speech model is not HF-signal aware. NR5 should preserve faint coherent signals while lowering stationary and slowly varying noise, with behavior suitable for SSB/CW weak-signal listening.

Initial design direction: build a new WDSP-native post-RXA stage that reuses EMNR's FFT/noise-estimation foundation and SBNR's psychoacoustic post-filter ideas, but adds a signal-preserving mask/salience gate so narrowband or slowly emerging signals are not over-subtracted. Keep it experimental until bench and on-air results show it actually improves copy over NR2/NR4.

Constraints: all NR modes are mutually exclusive in WDSP; the public NrMode enum is append-only because it is persisted; new DSP defaults are operator-visible and need maintainer sign-off; no new runtime dependency should be added without explicit approval.
design:
Proposed architecture:
1. Add a native WDSP stage (working name spnr/nr5) rather than composing NR2 and NR4 by enabling both, because RXA currently treats ANR/EMNR/RNNR/SBNR as mutually exclusive and RXAbp1Check/bp1 positioning depends on that model.
2. Reuse EMNR's overlap-add FFT frame shape, noise estimators, decision-directed a-priori SNR, and optional trained/zeta logic where practical.
3. Add signal-preservation logic before attenuation: track per-bin temporal coherence, local spectral peaks, and low-SNR persistence so faint carriers/CW tones/SSB formants can be held above the suppression floor.
4. Add a bounded post-filter inspired by SBNR/post2 to reduce musical noise without whitening away weak-signal identity.
5. Surface one conservative mode first (NR5 Experimental) with nullable advanced tunables later, following the NR2/NR4 lazy-default pattern.
6. Validate with synthetic IQ fixtures before UI polish: weak tone in AWGN, weak SSB-like modulated signal, fading carrier, impulsive noise with NB off/on, and silence/no-signal noise-floor stability.
acceptance_criteria:
- Design note documents algorithm, WDSP integration points, and expected tradeoffs versus NR2/NR4.
- Native stage builds on all supported platforms or degrades behind a compile-time flag/stub like NR3/NR4.
- C# P/Invoke layer can select NR5 without crashing when native symbols are missing.
- Existing NR1/NR2/NR4 mutual-exclusion tests are extended to include NR5.
- Synthetic weak-signal tests demonstrate NR5 preserves a buried tone or modulated signal better than the same chain with NR off, without broad clipping or instability.
- UI/persistence work is gated until the experimental backend is proven.

comments.author: Christian Suarez (N9WAR)

title: P2 [zeus] Bypass TX audio plugin chain (AudioChain) for TCI / remote TX audio sources
description:
Investigate + implement bypass of the entire TX audio plugin insert chain (the AudioChain / IAudioPlugin slots driven by AudioPluginBridge) when the active TX audio source is a remote TCI client (or any external/remote driver) instead of the local mic.

## Why
When a TCI client (ExpertSDR3, MSHV, remote tunnel users, etc.) supplies TX audio via the TCI binary stream, that audio has already been processed (or deliberately left clean) on the client side. Re-running the local Audio Suite plugins (EQ, compressor, noise gate, VST effects, etc.) produces double-processing, wrong timbre for voice, and broken behaviour for digital modes.

Today the TX plugin seam is source-agnostic: every block that reaches WdspDspEngine.ProcessTxBlock goes through the installed TxAudioBlockHandler → AudioChain, regardless of whether it came from browser mic, NativeMicCapture, WAV playback, or TciTxAudioReceiver.

## Current state (investigation summary)
- All TX audio sources funnel through TxAudioIngest (OnMicPcmBytesFromTci / FromMic / FromWav) with 500 ms hysteresis to keep sources from mixing.
- TxAudioIngest calls engine.ProcessTxBlock → WdspDspEngine (Zeus.Dsp/Wdsp/WdspDspEngine.cs:2417) does the volatile read of _txAudioPluginHandler and invokes AudioPluginBridge.Process if present.
- The handler always runs the _chain (TX AudioChain) before fexchange2. The chain only respects the global MasterBypassed + per-slot bypass today.
- TCI source awareness exists: TciSession._txSourceIsTci (set on TRX:... ,tci; command, cleared on plain trx or MOX fall). Exposed as TxSourceIsTci. Used only for TX_CHRONO pacing and accepting audio frames in that session. Not visible to the audio ingest or plugin bridge.
- Existing bypass (AudioChain.MasterBypassed via AudioChainMasterBypassService) is efficient (volatile + straight copy, no allocs, realtime safe) but is the operator's "Audio Suite" master toggle — not policy-driven by source.

See detailed findings in the session transcript for the investigation (key files: TxAudioIngest.cs, AudioPluginBridge.cs (the _chain vs _rxChain split), WdspDspEngine.cs:ProcessTxBlock + SetTxAudioPluginHandler, AudioChain.cs in Zeus.Plugins.Host, TciSession.cs:943 (the source flag), TciTxAudioReceiver.cs).

This is the TX-side counterpart to the RX post-demod plugin seam (zeus-3ar / feat/rx-audio-plugin-seam).

## Red-light / hot-path note
- Touches the TX audio hot path and the plugin seam (WdspDspEngine + DspPipelineService adjacent paths + AudioPluginBridge).
- Per CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md hot paths section + the zeus-3ar issue itself: this is red-light. Architecture + signal-routing change. Requires maintainer sign-off before merge. Do not land without explicit approval.
- Do not touch PureSignal paths or the recently-shipped audio dropout scheduling without separate approval.

## Design options (record decisions here)
1. Automatic override of master bypass when TCI source is active (simple, reuses everything).
2. Separate volatile flag on AudioChain (ExternalSourceBypass / BypassForRemote) composed with MasterBypassed. Cleanest policy vs. operator intent.
3. Swap the installed TxAudioBlockHandler to a pure passthrough when TCI source takes over.
4. Propagate source information (or "applyUserPlugins" bool) down to the handler / AudioBlockContext so the chain (or individual plugins) can decide.

Distinguish insert chain (the thing we want to bypass for remote) vs. ITxAudioTapPlugin taps (recorders etc. probably still want to observe raw + air audio).

Prefer option 2 or 3 for minimal pollution of the operator's master-bypass state.

The bypass must be bit-identical pass-through when engaged (same requirement as the existing master bypass ADR).

## Scope
- Make the bypass automatic / policy-driven when the authoritative TX source is TCI (or future "remote" sources).
- Keep the operator's Audio Suite master bypass as an independent override (or document the interaction clearly).
- Surface the state somewhere (capability? diagnostic? UI hint when a TCI session is driving TX?).
- Preserve behaviour for local mic and for WAV playback (WAV is "local" in intent).
- Taps vs. inserts: be explicit.
- Tests: unit tests around AudioChain + a source-aware path in TxAudioIngest / bridge (similar to existing AudioPluginBridgeProcessLivePreviewTests and AudioChainTests).
- On-air: verify on HL2 (user rig) that local mic still gets plugins, TCI-driven TX does not.
- Docs: update relevant lesson or TCI docs if the behaviour becomes user-visible.

## Non-goals (for this issue)
- Changing WDSP internal TXA stages.
- Adding new plugins or the UI for this bypass.
- Remote TCI control surface work (that's the tunnel epic).
- Altering the RX seam.

## References
- Current branch at investigation time: feat/rx-audio-plugin-seam (zeus-3ar)
- TCI TX audio path: Tci/TciTxAudioReceiver.cs, TxAudioIngest.cs:201 (OnMicPcmBytesFromTci)
- Plugin seam: AudioPluginBridge.cs:210 (SetTx...), 225 (the Process that calls chain), WdspDspEngine.cs:2417
- Bypass impl: AudioChain.cs:144 (if MasterBypassed copy), AudioChainMasterBypassService.cs
- TCI source flag: TciSession.cs:86, 192 (TxSourceIsTci), 943 (where it is set from the TRX command)
design: Options 1-4 recorded in description. Prefer separate ExternalSourceBypass volatile on AudioChain composed in Process, driven from TxAudioIngest's TCI recency / source flag. Or handler swap. Decision to be made in the issue after initial discussion. Do not mutate the operator-visible master bypass state without clear restore semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Automatic source-driven bypass of the TX insert plugin chain when TCI (or designated remote source) is supplying the audio; local mic and WAV playback continue to use the chain; operator master-bypass remains independent or clearly composed; bit-identical pass-through; hot-path safety preserved (no allocs/locks in audio thread); unit tests added; red-light maintainer sign-off obtained before merge; verified on HL2 that behaviour differs correctly by source.

Add MoxSource.Plugin=4 with same ownership/release semantics as Cwx. Fold into existing host PR #590 (feat/rx-audio-plugin-seam) as a separate commit. Red-light (TX control) — needs Brian signoff.

title: RTTY TX (continuous-phase AFSK + plugin PTT via IRadioController)
description: Generate continuous-phase AFSK from typed text -> Baudot -> UART, key TX via IRadioController.SetMoxAsync, inject through IAudioPlaybackSink.PlayOnAir (frames paced). Diddle/idle LTRS. Depends on RTTY RX (shared Baudot/AFSK code) and the host RadioController wiring.

Loopback test all-pass (CQ, figures, reverse, DWD config). Live: keyed + sent 63 chars, ~11.8s key-down matches 45.45 baud. Uncommitted on feat/rtty-plugin.

title: RTTY RX demod plugin (Baudot/AFSK, RX audio tap)
description: Pure RX-audio-tap plugin (IRxAudioTapPlugin). Mark/space tone detection (Goertzel) at 2125/2295 Hz, bit-clock recovery at 45.45 baud, UART framing (1 start, 5 data, 1.5 stop), Baudot/ITA2 decode with LTRS/FIGS + USOS. UI panel: decoded text, mark/space tuning indicator, AFC. No host change. Green-light.

Working config: mark 750 / shift 450 / baud 50 / REV OFF (USB polarity needed REV off, not on). Fixed light-theme invisible-text bug in rtty.tsx (hardcoded terminal colors). DWD tones landed ~750/1200 Hz in audio with operator's CW-ish tuning; detector follows via Mark Hz.

title: RTTY plugin (RX + TX) for Zeus
description: A RTTY (Baudot/ITA2 AFSK-over-SSB) plugin: receive (decode mark/space FSK to text) and transmit (generate continuous-phase AFSK, key TX via plugin radio control, inject via playback sink). RX is green-light (pure RX audio tap, no host change). TX depends on wiring IRadioController in the host (folded into PR #590) so a plugin can key MOX like the CW keyer does (MoxSource). Standard AFSK 2125/2295 Hz, 170 Hz shift, 45.45 baud.

title: RX audio plugin insert seam (rx.post-demod) + CW SCAF filter
description:
Add a host-side RX audio plugin insert chain so plugins declaring the rx.post-demod manifest slot can shape demodulated RX (band) audio — counterpart to the existing TX-only WDSP plugin seam. First consumer is a CW SCAF (switched-capacitor-style variable-bandwidth CW audio filter).

WHY: operators want a tunable narrow CW audio filter (outboard-SCAF equivalent) on RX. The audio-plugin contract (IAudioPlugin/AudioChain) and the rx.post-demod manifest+schema slot already exist; only the host runtime wiring was missing.

WHAT (two repos, both on feature branches, committed no-push):
- openhpsdr-zeus @ feat/rx-audio-plugin-seam (872de85): DspPipelineService.SetRxAudioPluginHandler invoked in place over the 48 kHz mono RX block in Tick() after MOX fade, before CW sidetone mix (null handler => RX path bit-identical). AudioPluginBridge routes rx.* slots into a separate _rxChain (own instances + IIR state, not in ChainOrderService).
- openhpsdr-zeus-plugins @ feat/cw-scaf-rx-filter (f01f703): audio/CwScaf — ScafDsp (cascade of 1-4 RBJ 0 dB-peak bandpass biquads), CwScafPlugin (REST params/meters + persistence), React panel with live response curve.
design: Engine-agnostic: seam is host-side in DspPipelineService, works for P1 and P2 RX. RX chain master-bypass NOT wired to the Audio Suite master (per-plugin Bypass works); no dedicated RX Audio Tools window (panel renders standalone under Plugins). Filter is in-place: _rxChain.Process(audio, audio, ctx) is safe because AudioChain seeds output from input then ping-pongs output<->scratch, never re-reading input.
acceptance_criteria: MAINTAINER SIGN-OFF REQUIRED before merge: touches DspPipelineService (hot path) and reverses the 2026-04-30 TX-only audio-chain decision. Build 0/0 and audio tests green (32+18). Verified on Hermes-Lite 2: CW SCAF on live RX audio shows ~17 dB out-of-band rejection (inputPeak ~0.42 -> outputPeak ~0.06). Open a discussion issue per CONTRIBUTING before raising the PR.
notes: Draft PRs opened (gated on Brian/EI6LF signoff): host seam = Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus#590 (RX insert + taps + playback sink); plugins = Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus-plugins#27 (CW SCAF + full Recorder). Both target develop, cross-referenced. Hot-path (DspPipelineService) + architecture (reverses TX-only audio chain) = red-light, do not merge without maintainer review.

title: [zeus] Multi-tenant TCP relay: per-operator port+secret for remote TCI
description:
Scale the single-user raw-TCP tunnel (zeus-dl6 / zeus-e5e) to a multi-tenant relay so several Zeus operators share one public relay host, each with their OWN remote port AND OWN secret, isolated from each other (operator A's secret cannot open operator B's port / radio).

WHY NOT bore: stock bore has a SINGLE global --secret shared by all clients; any authenticated client can grab any free port. No per-port/per-secret mapping. Running N bore instances per user is impractical — bore's control port is fixed at 7835 and the client always dials it, so you cannot host multiple bore servers behind one IP with distinct control ports.

This is broker territory and converges with the openhpsdrzeus.com broker (zeus-mi5), EXCEPT the relay itself cannot live on that broker: it is a Cloudflare Worker and Workers cannot listen on raw TCP. So the relay must run on a VPS; the web app only manages identities and hands out (port, secret) pairs.
design:
Two viable designs:

OPTION A — frp + small auth web app (off-the-shelf relay, no fork):
frp (fast reverse proxy) supports many frpc clients on one frps, plus a server-plugin system: on each Login/NewProxy registration frps makes an HTTP call to YOUR app to authorize/deny and to pin the remote_port. The small web app holds the operator -> (port, secret) table and answers yes/no. frps does the TCP relay. Standard tunnel-as-a-service pattern. Least code.

OPTION B — native .NET relay (owns both ends, fits Zeus):
Write the relay server in .NET to pair with the native C# tunnel client from zeus-e5e. Client presents (port, secret); server validates against a table (LiteDB/SQL), binds that remote port, proxies bytes. The 'small web app' is the operator-registration panel that issues port+secret. No external daemon; converges with the broker (broker = identity + port/secret issuance on the Worker, relay = VPS .NET service).

Recommendation: Option A if we want something working soon and robust; Option B if this should be an end-to-end Zeus product with full protocol control (reuses the C# client spike).
notes: Context: single-operator self-hosted bore server already validated working on an Ubuntu x86_64 VPS this session (see zeus-e5e notes). This issue is the multi-operator follow-on. Decision A-vs-B still open.

title: [zeus] Spike: native C# bore client (BoreTunnelService) for TCI raw-TCP tunnel
description:
Investigate and prototype a NATIVE C# bore client embedded in Zeus.Server.Hosting (BoreTunnelService) to expose the TCI server (:40001) over a raw-TCP reverse tunnel, instead of bundling the bore binary as a supervised subprocess. This is the recommended approach from zeus-dl6 (the TCI remote-access gap). Living doc — log all bore findings here.

Why native C# over bundling the binary: avoids shipping/signing a third-party executable (no equivalent of zeus-unr macOS notarize / zeus-vo5 Windows Authenticode just for the tunnel), runs anywhere .NET runs, and keeps lifecycle/health/public-URL surfacing fully in-process (no subprocess supervisor / stdout parsing like zeus-b01). Fits Zeus's existing hosted-service pattern.

Open questions to resolve in this spike:
- Pin bore protocol/wire format from source (github.com/ekzhang/bore) and decide version handling.
- Where does the bore SERVER live? bore.pub (free, shared, no-auth, port-collision risk) for dev; self-hosted 'bore server --secret' on a free-tier VPS for prod. NOTE: cannot live on the openhpsdrzeus.com broker — it's a Cloudflare Worker and Workers cannot listen on raw TCP.
- Config surface: server host, control port, requested remote port, secret -> prefs + Server tab.
- Security: HMAC secret on the data path; the public endpoint otherwise lets anyone control the radio.
design:
bore protocol (client side) to reimplement — verify exact wire format against source before coding:
- Connect TCP to server control port (bore.pub default 7835).
- JSON-delimited control messages: Hello(port) to request remote port (0=any, or 40001); if server requires a secret it sends Challenge(uuid) and client replies Authenticate(HMAC-SHA256(secret, uuid)); server confirms assigned port; periodic Heartbeat keepalive.
- Per inbound connection: server sends Connection(uuid); client opens a NEW TCP to the control port, sends Accept(uuid), then bidirectionally proxies bytes between that socket and localhost:40001.
Implementation: ~200-300 lines using TcpClient + System.Text.Json + HMACSHA256, one Task per accepted connection copying streams both ways. Expose current public host:port via typed API for the Server tab. Since TCI control AND audio (RxAudioStream/TxAudioStream binary frames) share the single :40001 WebSocket, the byte-transparent relay carries everything in one connection (see zeus-dl6).
notes:
bore VALIDATED end-to-end this session (2026-06-01) with the stock binary before any C# work.

PUBLIC bore.pub:
- 'bore local 40001 --to bore.pub' -> bore.pub:46264 (random). Plain-TCP connect OPEN; ws:// (no TLS) upgrade returned 101 Switching Protocols (Server: Kestrel) + full TCI handshake (protocol:ExpertSDR3,2.0; device:Zeus; ...).
- 'bore local 40001 --to bore.pub --port 40001' SUCCEEDED -> bore.pub:40001 (fixed port granted because free). Re-verified 101 + handshake. Useful because the TCI client pins port 40001.
- Caveats: bore.pub fixed port is best-effort (fails if held); no auth on the bore.pub data path (anyone with host:port controls the radio).

SELF-HOSTED server VALIDATED (Ubuntu x86_64 VPS):
- Install: github release v0.6.0 x86_64-unknown-linux-musl binary -> /usr/local/bin/bore.
- systemd unit (User=bore system account, EnvironmentFile=/etc/bore.env holding BORE_SECRET from 'openssl rand -hex 32', chmod 600). ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/bore server (dropped --min/--max-port; security = secret + VPS firewall opening only 7835 control + 40001 tunnel). bore reads BORE_SECRET from env.
- Client (Mac): 'bore local 40001 --to VPS_IP --port 40001 --secret <SECRET>'. User confirmed it works correctly.
- LESSON (process): long single-line shell commands wrap in the chat pane and the user's mobile SSH client injects 2 leading spaces at wrap points, corrupting heredocs/base64 on paste. Keep emitted command lines SHORT; split into one-liners.

Client config: WSJT-X/JTDX Rig='Expert Electronics TCI', Network Server='HOST:40001'.

NEXT (multi-tenant): per-operator port+secret tracked in zeus-n99 (stock bore = single global secret only).

title: [zeus] Remote TCI access needs a raw-TCP tunnel — Cloudflare/JWT design can't serve it
description:
GAP: the tunnel epic (zeus-mi5) exposes ONLY port 6060 (web UI + SignalR hub) via Cloudflare Quick Tunnel (cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:6060, see zeus-b01), over HTTPS/WSS and gated by Google OAuth + JWT validated on the WebSocket connect (zeus-86w). The TCI server on :40001 is not in scope of any bead (bd search tci = 0 results).

This is structurally incompatible with real TCI clients (WSJT-X/JTDX via Hamlib 'Expert Electronics TCI', Log4OM, N1MM): they only open a plain ws://host:port — no TLS (wss), no OAuth, no JWT, and per the user's constraint cannot run any helper/sidecar on the client machine. cloudflared CANNOT carry raw TCP by design (HTTP/WS only); Tailscale Funnel is likewise TLS-only. So the TCI leg needs a SEPARATE raw-TCP tunnel binary, supervised the same way zeus-b01 supervises cloudflared, pointed at :40001.

Use case: operator runs Zeus+HL2 at home behind a Google Wifi mesh (no router admin, no port-forward) and wants WSJT-X on a remote machine to drive the rig over TCI.
design:
Recommendation: bundle 'bore' (github.com/ekzhang/bore, MIT, single static Rust binary) as a supervised subprocess analogous to zeus-b01, pointed at localhost:40001, requesting a FIXED remote port so the client config is stable. 'bore.pub' is a free public server (no account) but has no auth on the data path — anyone with host:port controls the radio; mitigate with self-hosted 'bore server --secret' on a free-tier VPS (e.g. Oracle Always Free) for a permanent, secret-gated host:port. Alternatives compared: ngrok TCP (works, but fixed address is paid; free = dynamic, forces client reconfig every restart); FRP/SSH -R to a free-tier VPS (stable, free, more setup); Tailscale (stable 100.x IP, raw TCP, free — but requires Tailscale on the CLIENT, which is excluded here).

Key enabler verified in code: TCI audio rides the SAME WebSocket/connection as control — RxAudioStream=1/TxAudioStream=2/IqStream=0 are binary frames with a 64-byte header on :40001 (Zeus.Server.Hosting/Tci/TciStreamPayload.cs). A byte-transparent raw-TCP relay therefore carries control AND audio in one connection; no separate audio path needed. Bandwidth note: RX audio = 48kHz float32 stereo approx 3 Mbit/s upstream; do NOT enable the IQ stream (192kHz) over a remote link.
notes:
TESTS RUN THIS SESSION (2026-06-01, Zeus web mode on :6060, TCI on :40001, HL2 connected):

1. Cloudflare NAMED tunnel (existing ~/.cloudflared/zeus-morrotel.yml: tci.morrotel.eu -> http://localhost:40001) DOES expose TCI as wss://tci.morrotel.eu on 443. Verified: WS upgrade forcing HTTP/1.1 returned '101 Switching Protocols' + full TCI handshake (protocol:ExpertSDR3,2.0; device:Zeus; vfo/modulation/drive...). Over HTTP/2 it returns 400 — a curl artifact (Upgrade header invalid in h2), NOT a tunnel fault. BUT wss is unusable by plain TCI clients, so this does not solve the use case.

2. Firewall ruled out: macOS Application Firewall disabled (socketfilterfw state=0, no block-all, no stealth); no third-party firewall (LuLu/Little Snitch/Murus) process, kext, or system extension. ANOMALY (unresolved, local-only): from the host itself, 127.0.0.1:40001 and the Tailscale IP 100.75.236.17:40001 connect fine, but the en0 LAN IP 192.168.86.21:40001 is CLOSED. Socket is bound IPv6 *:40001 (dual-stack). Not chased further per user; would block any future router port-forward to the LAN IP.

3. ngrok TCP works end-to-end: 'ngrok tcp 40001' -> tcp://2.tcp.eu.ngrok.io:13853 -> localhost:40001. Verified plain-TCP connect OPEN and a plain ws:// (no TLS) upgrade returned '101 Switching Protocols' (Server: Kestrel) + the TCI handshake. Confirms a byte-transparent raw-TCP relay is the correct shape. Rejected by user only for the dynamic address (free tier).

4. playit.gg considered: fixed address is paid. Router port-forward impossible (Google Wifi mesh, no admin password).

title: AI net monitor: transcribe a frequency and report who was on + topics discussed
description:
Leave Zeus tuned to a frequency (e.g. 7.222 MHz) for hours and get back an AI-generated report: a roster of who was on (callsigns, QRZ-enriched names/locations), a topic/timeline summary, and a searchable transcript. An "AI net logger / frequency monitor." Genuinely novel for ham SDR — ASR-on-radio exists in the scanner/trunking world (trunk-recorder + Whisper, OpenMHz) but nobody integrates ASR + callsign extraction + QRZ enrichment + LLM summarization into the SDR itself for HF SSB nets.

Most of the pipeline is straightforward in this stack; ONE part is the real risk (ASR on HF SSB). Plan as two tracks: (1) plumbing, (2) an ASR-quality spike that must pass before committing to track 1.

PIPELINE (and difficulty):
1. Audio capture — EASY. Tap demodulated RX audio, same seam as the CW decoder (zeus CW work / #548) and the proposed SSTV decode.
2. Transmission segmentation — MEDIUM. Detect key-up/key-down / signal-present boundaries; each "over" = one station's turn (better basis for who-spoke than acoustic diarization on a single noisy HF channel).
3. Speech-to-text — HARD (make-or-break). Whisper (whisper.cpp local; M4 Pro runs it well). HF SSB throws QSB/noise/splatter/weak-signal/global-accents/phonetics/Q-codes at it; Whisper hallucinates text on silence/noise, so VAD gating is mandatory. Callsigns are the worst case (not dictionary words).
4. Callsign extraction — MEDIUM. Parse transcript for IDs, decode phonetics ("whiskey one alpha" -> W1A...), then cross-reference QRZ (already integrated) to enrich AND validate the ASR guess against a real callsign.
5. Summarization — EASY/high-value. LLM (Claude API) over the transcript -> topics, participants, timeline, notable items.
6. Report + live view — EASY. Web frontend: building roster, live transcript pane, "generate report" button, searchable archive, reachable from phone.

HONEST EXPECTATION: "surprisingly useful summary with imperfect details," NOT court-transcript accuracy. Plenty for "who was on and roughly what was discussed"; not a verbatim log.

WHY IT FITS ZEUS: audio-tap seam already proven (CW decoder); QRZ integration already exists for enrichment+validation; web/phone frontend for live view and reports; long-running networked backend is exactly right for unattended multi-hour monitoring. Composes with the decode-everything and DXCC-overlay ideas into a situational-awareness layer.

RESPONSIBLE FLAG: amateur transmissions are not private (US Part 97), so personal monitoring/logging is fine. Be thoughtful about PUBLISHING identified transcripts of named individuals — etiquette varies and other countries' rules differ. Default stance: personal-use, think before publishing.

ARCHITECTURE SKETCH: a backend service (Zeus.NetMonitor or similar) or IBackendPlugin+IUiPlugin: RX audio tap -> VAD/transmission segmenter -> per-over buffer -> ASR (whisper.cpp) -> callsign extractor + QRZ enrich -> store segments (LiteDB) -> LLM summarizer on demand/rolling -> SignalR push to frontend.
acceptance_criteria:
PHASE 0 — ASR-quality spike (gate; do this FIRST, before any plumbing):
- Record ~30 min of a real HF SSB net (e.g. 7.222) off the radio.
- Run through whisper.cpp with a ham-context prompt (phonetics, Q-codes, callsign hints).
- Assess: is transcription good enough that callsign extraction + summarization are viable? Document WER/qualitative findings.
- GO/NO-GO decision recorded on this issue before Phase 1.

PHASE 1 — Decode/monitor plumbing (only if Phase 0 is GO):
- RX audio tap feeding a monitor service (pattern on the CW decoder seam).
- Transmission/VAD segmentation produces per-over audio segments.
- ASR transcribes segments; results stored with timestamps.
- Live transcript streams to a frontend panel over SignalR.

PHASE 2 — Enrichment + report:
- Callsign extraction with phonetic decoding; QRZ cross-reference for name/location + validation.
- LLM summarization produces: roster (callsigns+names), topic timeline, notable items.
- Frontend: live roster + transcript, "generate report", searchable archive, phone-accessible.
- Personal-use default; no auto-publish of identified transcripts.
notes: 2026-06-12: Phases 1-3 MERGED to develop (PRs #601/#602/#604) + fix #605; in next nightly. Phase 3 = report/roster/search + per-over audio replay, local-LLM digest (llama-completion child proc, $0/offline), and button-download engine bundles. Engines NOT baked into app (multi-dylib); built static/self-contained per-RID by .github/workflows/build-voyeur-engines.yml (on main via #603/#606), published to release tag voyeur-engines-v1, fetched on demand by in-app setup. osx-arm64 bundles built+published+VERIFIED end-to-end locally (download->extract->run). CI building other 5 RIDs (run 27450402790; win-arm64 failed first pass, iterating). Issue stays OPEN until develop->main release ships. HL2 RX-unaffected bench proof still owed.

title: Design doc: HL2 VNA mode support (docs/designs/)
description:
Write a design document at docs/designs/hl2-vna-mode.md for adding Hermes Lite 2 VNA (vector network analyzer) support to Zeus. Research is DONE (see notes/design fields) — this issue is only to author the design doc; implementation will follow as separate issues once the doc is reviewed by the maintainer (red-light feature: new architecture + new UI).

Scope of the doc: capture the gateware mechanism, exact Protocol-1 register mapping (all already-written registers in Zeus), the FPGA-scan sweep procedure, the measurement/calibration model, the hardware caveats, and the proposed Zeus seams (Protocol1, Server VnaService, Contracts DTOs, frontend Smith chart). Recommend a phased rollout starting with an HL2-verified spike.
design:
RESEARCH COMPLETE — facts for docs/designs/hl2-vna-mode.md.

GATEWARE (softerhardware/Hermes-Lite2, control.v + radio.v):
- VNA enable: reg 0x09, cmd_data[23] = C2 bit 7 (0x80). Confirmed: vna <= cmd_data[23] at cmd_addr 6h09.
- VNA count (N points): reg 0x09, cmd_data[15:0] = C3:C4.
- TX drive: reg 0x09, cmd_data[31:24] = C1 (Zeus already writes here).
- VNA fixed RX gain: reg 0x00 bit 10 (0=-6dB, 1=+6dB).
- Duplex/1RX/48k: reg 0x00 bits [2]/[6:3]/[25:24].
- Start freq: reg 0x01 (Zeus CcRegister.TxFreq, wire 0x02), uint32 Hz.
- Delta Hz/point: reg 0x02 (Zeus CcRegister.RxFreq, wire 0x04), uint32 Hz.
- MOX=1 required (FPGA-scan). TX/RX share NCO -> each output IQ sample = vector ratio at that freq. PA + internal T/R relay disabled in VNA (~vna).
notes:
ALL needed registers ALREADY WRITTEN by Zeus — only frame CONTENT changes, no wire-format/new-register work.

FPGA-SCAN: set count/start/delta; FPGA steps NCO, averages 1024 CORDIC samples/point, emits 1 IQ sample/point, prepends ONE zero IQ sample as block separator. Host re-syncs on the zero. Results arrive as normal RX0 IqFrames (Zeus pipeline already handles).

MEASUREMENT/CAL: M(f)=I+jQ raw vector ratio, needs cal.
- S11/SWR: needs EXTERNAL resistive return-loss bridge + 6-10dB attenuators. OSL cal. SWR=(1+|G|)/(1-|G|), RL=-20log10|G|, Z=50(1+G)/(1-G).
- S21 (filters): TX->DUT->RX + attenuators, thru cal, NO bridge -> good first target.

CAVEATS (load-bearing): measures at low-power ports NOT antenna-through-PA; never TX->RX direct (ADC damage); phase only stable while VNA held -> recal per power cycle, keep VNA on whole session; MUST clear VNA bit on disconnect or it blocks reconnection.

PROPOSED SEAMS:
- Zeus.Protocol1: RadioControlState/CcState add VnaEnabled/VnaPoints/VnaStepHz; ControlFrame.WriteCcBytes set bit10@Config + C2bit7+count@DriveFilter(0x09); Protocol1Client sweep mode diverts IqFrames to VNA consumer.
- Zeus.Server.Hosting: VnaService orchestrates sweep + OSL/thru cal + SWR/RL/Z compute; DTOs in Zeus.Contracts; REST or WS frame.
- Frontend: Smith chart + SWR/RL plot + O/S/L cal flow.

ROLLOUT: doc -> HL2-verified spike (enable VNA + sweep + dump raw N points to log, confirm bit mapping + zero-separator on real hw) -> S21 -> S11 w/OSL -> UI.

SOURCES: HL2 Protocol wiki; HL1 Protocol-Coverage; Quisk SetVNA() (IW0HDV/quisk hermes/quisk_hardware.py); N2ADR VNA reflection-mode google group; EA4GPZ destevez.net mains-choke VNA post.

RED-LIGHT: new architecture + UI -> doc reviewed by maintainer (Brian EI6LF) before impl. Bit mapping bench-verified on HL2 before UI.
close_reason: Design doc written at docs/designs/hl2-vna-mode.md, PR #574 against develop

title: feat(cw): unify CW Keyer + CW Decoder into one panel (terminal + TX text line + threshold scope)
description:
Merge the two existing CW panels — CW Keyer ('cw': CwPanel.tsx/CwKeyer.tsx, WPM/keyer-mode/sidetone/macros + /api/cw/send|abort, status frame 0x30) and CW Decoder ('cwdecoder': CwDecoderPanel.tsx/CwDecoder.tsx, decoded-text frame 0x31) — into a SINGLE unified CW panel. This is the operator-requested consolidation; the two-panel split is confusing.

Unified panel layout (operator's design intent — visual treatment is RED-LIGHT, needs Brian/EI6LF sign-off):
1. SETTINGS/SLIDERS region: keep existing keyer controls (WPM, keyer mode STR/IAM-A/IAM-B, sidetone pitch/gain, macros) — these already round-trip via /api/cw/settings.
2. TERMINAL region: an upper window that shows decoded RX text (from 0x31), and a SINGLE free-text input line at the bottom that the operator types into and transmits (wire to existing /api/cw/send). One terminal, decode-up / type-and-send-down.
3. THRESHOLD SCOPE region: a small audio/envelope view that plots the RX CW envelope with a horizontal THRESHOLD line the operator drags with the mouse to sit just above the noise floor — so noise stops jittering the WPM estimate. Requires backend work: CwAudioDecoder already computes _env/_noiseFloor/_signal and Schmitt hi/lo internally (CwAudioDecoder.cs ~149-171) but does NOT expose them. Need (a) a telemetry path (envelope samples + current noiseFloor/signal) to the frontend, and (b) a way to set a MANUAL threshold that overrides the adaptive Schmitt trigger.

RED-LIGHT items (all need maintainer sign-off before merge, per CLAUDE.md):
- Visual design / layout / unification of the two panels (UX behavior change).
- Wire-format: new envelope-telemetry frame (propose MsgType 0x32) + manual-threshold field/endpoint. Prefer extending /api/cw/settings + a new telemetry frame over reshaping 0x31.
- Panel registration change: collapsing two registered panels ('cw' + 'cwdecoder') into one affects saved layouts — migration/back-compat needs a decision.

Approach: use the frontend-design skill to produce the unified-panel design first (mockup + component plan), get maintainer sign-off, THEN implement backend telemetry + frontend. Build incrementally; do not touch keyer timing, sidetone generation, or TX IQ path.

Supersedes the earlier narrow scope (decode our own TX keying), which folds into the unified terminal's TX line.
design: Reuse Morse FSM from CwAudioDecoder (factor out from the envelope detector — MorseDecode dict + FSM live at CwAudioDecoder.cs:71/247). Source = keyer key-on/off events from CwEngine.cs / CwSidetoneSource.cs (raw-key path), NOT audio. Extend CwDecodedTextFrame (0x31) with an origin/direction flag rather than a new MsgType. Files: Zeus.Server.Hosting/CwEngine.cs, CwSidetoneSource.cs, CwAudioDecoder.cs, CwDecoderService.cs; Zeus.Contracts/CwDecodedTextFrame.cs; zeus-web/src/state/cw-decoder-store.ts, components/design/CwDecoder.tsx, layout/panels/CwDecoderPanel.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: One CW panel replaces both 'cw' and 'cwdecoder'. It exposes the existing keyer settings (WPM, keyer mode, sidetone, macros), a terminal with RX decode above and a free-text TX input line below (wired to /api/cw/send), and an envelope scope with a mouse-draggable threshold line that, when set, pins the decoder's key-detection threshold (stopping noise-driven WPM jitter). Adaptive mode still works when no manual threshold is set. Saved layouts referencing the old panel ids migrate gracefully. All visual/UX/wire-format changes are called out for maintainer sign-off in the PR. Headless-safe; dark+light themes both correct; tokens.css colors only (no new hardcoded hex).

title: Audio Chain Monitor: useChainFocus deeplink hook + control registration
description: zeus-web/src/hooks/use-chain-focus.ts. Widget's Show me button posts a chain-focus event with a stable control ID (e.g. 'tx.mic-gain'); each settings panel that owns a relevant control registers itself + scrolls/highlights on receipt. 1.5s --accent outline pulse on focus. Acceptance: clicking Show me on the Mic-low verdict opens TX Audio Settings, scrolls to Mic Gain slider, pulses it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor: Factory Widget (Direction A — horizontal pipeline)
description: Implement the Audio Chain Monitor Directions handoff bundle's Direction A: left-to-right pipeline of 9 stage tiles (7 WDSP TXA + Wire + PA) with WDSP/Wire/PA group spacing, advisory rail beneath with worst-severity verdict, Show me + Apply + Dismiss buttons. Reuses existing Zeus tokens (--panel-top/bot, --accent, --tx, --power, Archivo Narrow). Hard rule: NO amber #FFA028. NO inline sliders on tiles. NO autopilot UI. Place panel in workspace as a dockable widget. Renders against the AudioChainHealth store + existing TxStore (TxMetersV2). Pixel-faithful to the prototype HTML in the handoff bundle.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor: frontend frame decode + store
description: zeus-web/src/realtime/ws-client.ts += MSG_TYPE_AUDIO_CHAIN_HEALTH = 0x32 dispatch. New zeus-web/src/state/audio-chain-health-store.ts (zustand) carrying the latest verdict map keyed by StageId, session-only dismissals, and selectors. Types matching Zeus.Contracts/AudioChainHealthFrame.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor: apply endpoint dispatcher
description: Maps a Verdict's ApplyTarget back onto an existing backend setter (mic gain, leveler max-gain, CFC drive, comp threshold, alc max, drive level, etc.). Most setters exist already for the corresponding settings panels — this is wiring not new endpoints. POST /api/audio-chain/apply { stageId, applyTarget } evaluated against a small registry. Rule engine computes absolute target values (per ADR-0003), dispatcher is the executor.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor: per-context overrides (mode + board)
description: Per-(mode, board-class) override classes layered on the base rule set, mirroring IRadioDriveProfile / PaDefaults pattern. v1 overrides: HL2 (drive 0-15 range, lower PA wattage targets), Digital modes (invert verdicts on Leveler/CFC/Comp — active processing = warn), FM (most TXA stages → info), CW (TXA group → info entirely), AM (ALC headroom shift). Acceptance: rule evaluator selects the right override for (mode, ConnectedBoardKind) without if-trees scattered through rule code.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor: base rule set (seed thresholds from Thetis recipes)
description: Hard-coded base rule set in AudioChainHealthRules.cs covering the 13 stages. v1 seed rules: Mic low/hot, EQ clip, Leveler GR runaway, CFC pumping, Comp pumping, ALC clip+immediate, Wire drive ceiling, PA fwd<expected, PA SWR>2 (warn) / >3 (error+immediate), PA volts/amps anomalies on boards that report them. Each rule carries provenance (Thetis source reference) in a code comment. Acceptance: at least 12 distinct rules across stages.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor: rule engine (sustained-violation + hysteresis state)
description: VerdictEvaluator with per-rule state: sustained-violation window (3 s warn / 5 s error defaults, overridable) + hysteresis band on level rules (2 dB default). Stateless threshold check + small ring per active rule. Pulls reading + context (mode, board, MOX) and returns a Verdict or null. Unit tests for windowing edges and hysteresis flutter.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor: service skeleton (2 Hz tick, broadcast pipeline)
description: AudioChainHealthService : IHostedService in Zeus.Server.Hosting. 2 Hz tick, evaluates rules against live TxMetersV2/PaTemp/RadioService snapshot, broadcasts AudioChainHealth frame. Hook into existing WS broadcast pipeline alongside TxMetersService. Empty rule set OK for this issue; rule eval is a stub returning all-OK.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor: wire contracts (AudioChainHealthFrame + MsgType 0x32)
description: Add Zeus.Contracts/AudioChainHealthFrame.cs (Verdict record, Severity enum, StageId taxonomy, frame serialiser), Zeus.Contracts/MsgType.cs += AudioChainHealth = 0x32, and round-trip tests. No service logic. Stable wire contract; renaming a StageId is a wire break.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audio Chain Monitor (epic)
description: Core diagnostic service that visualises the TX audio chain from mic to power-out as a 'factory pipeline' with per-stage verdicts and a one-click Apply path. Replaces tribal knowledge — instead of 'ask the expert', the operator sees at a glance which stage is misbehaving and is shown which slider to adjust. Architecture decided in CONTEXT.md and docs/adr/0001-0003. Shape: 13 stages (7 WDSP TXA + 1 wire collapsed + 1 PA collapsed), 4-state severity (ok/info/warn/error with immediate-action flag), AudioChainHealth WS frame (0x32) at 2 Hz carrying verdicts only, base-rule-set with per-mode/per-board overrides matching IRadioDriveProfile pattern. Diagnose + one-click Apply, no autopilot. Direction A (horizontal pipeline) from the design handoff bundle.
close_reason: Closed

title: Analog S-meter: top padding truncates '7' label
description:
In the Analog S-METER panel, the top of the dial arc is clipped — the '7' label on the upper scale is being truncated by the panel's top edge. Needs additional top padding/margin (or reduced arc radius) inside the meter SVG/container so the highest tick labels render fully.

Repro: open S-METER panel in Analog style → observe '7' label cut off at top.

Fix area: zeus-web analog S-meter component (likely AnalogSMeter / meter SVG viewBox or container padding-top).

title: [mobile] QR scanner + /go/<slug> redirect handling
description: Mobile scans the QR on Zeus desktop, extracts the openhpsdrzeus.com/go/<slug> URL, and follows the broker's 302 redirect to the current trycloudflare URL while presenting its sealed-cookie session. Mobile remembers the slug for subsequent launches so the user does not re-scan. Handle 'desktop offline' (broker returns a no-tunnel response) gracefully — show 'your shack is offline' rather than a broken redirect.

title: [mobile] Google OAuth login on first install (broker session)
description: Per ADR 0003, mobile pairing relies on the same Google account being signed in on both Zeus desktop and the mobile app. zeus-mobile (Capacitor) needs a first-run flow that authenticates the user against openhpsdrzeus.com's existing OAuth endpoints (66b2c13) and stores the sealed-cookie session locally. Subsequent app launches reuse the session without re-prompting.

title: [zeus] Bundle cloudflared.exe in Windows installer with Authenticode
description: Per ADR 0004, the Windows installer must include cloudflared.exe Authenticode-signed alongside Zeus's own binaries. Update release.yml to download a pinned cloudflared version at build time, sign it, and include it in the MSI/EXE installer payload. Document the installer-size impact (~30 MB).

title: [zeus] Bundle cloudflared in macOS .app and notarize
description: Per ADR 0004, the macOS bundle must include cloudflared inside Zeus.app/Contents/Resources/ (or MacOS/), code-signed and notarized as part of the existing Apple flow. Cloudflare ships unsigned tar.gz releases — the Zeus build pipeline downloads a pinned version at build time, re-signs, and notarizes with the rest of the bundle. Update create-macos-app.sh and zeus-sign-notarize.sh to learn about the embedded binary.

title: [zeus] Server tab: Go Online toggle + QR rendering
description: Extends the Cloudflare Tunnel opt-in card landed in ce1c37e on feature/tunnel. Render a Go Online toggle that spawns cloudflared, displays the stable openhpsdrzeus.com/go/<slug> URL, and shows a scannable QR code for that URL. Surface live status (registering / online / heartbeat-failing / offline). No raw trycloudflare.com URL in the UI — the broker indirection is the operator-facing contract.

title: [zeus] JWKS validator + JWT auth on WebSocket connect
description: Per ADR 0002, every WebSocket connect to Zeus from a paired mobile must present a broker-minted Ed25519 JWT in the Authorization header.
